title: lappula subcaespitosa
intro: Lappula subcaespitosa, also known as small bugloss, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and fever. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Lappula subcaespitosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
